title: ADF Beam: Confluence QR to ADFReader

questions.prompt: What is the end-to-end acceptance bar — the demo that proves this goal is done?
questions.description: This becomes the top-line validation fact.
questions.recommendedAnswer: On a physical iPhone running ADFReader, scan the animated QR shown by the extension on a real Confluence page (e.g., the 2026-06-03 meeting notes page) and see it rendered correctly in the app.

options.label: Real iPhone + real Confluence page, end-to-end scan and render
Simulator with pasted chunk payloads is enough (no device required)
Both: automated round-trip tests must pass AND real-device scan must work

questions.prompt: Which automated checks should gate completion?
questions.recommendedAnswer: Swift unit tests for chunk reassembly + inflate round-trip against fixtures; an extension-side round-trip test page proving chunk → QR → decode → reassemble equals the original ADF.

options.label: Swift tests: chunk reassembly + decompress round-trip on fixtures
Extension test page: compress/chunk/encode round-trip in browser
Shared protocol fixtures: same chunk payloads verified on both sides

questions.prompt: Should scan time be part of acceptance criteria, and at what bar?
questions.recommendedAnswer: A typical (~50 KB ADF) page completes a scan in under 10 seconds under normal conditions.

options.label: Typical page scans in under 10 seconds
No timing criteria — correctness and feedback only
Stricter: typical page under 5 seconds, include large-page bar too

questions.prompt: Include live tuning controls (fps / chunk size) in the extension overlay for v1?
questions.recommendedAnswer: Yes to fps slider (cheap, helps find reliable scan rate on your monitor); fixed 800-byte chunk size for v1.

options.label: fps control only, fixed chunk size

questions.prompt: Confirm scope boundaries (inferred from our design discussion).
questions.description: In: MV3 unpacked extension under Tools/, Confluence Cloud only (*.atlassian.net), toolbar-triggered overlay, ADFReader scan entry with progress/haptics/success, simulator paste-injection dev path. Out: Chrome Web Store publishing, Confluence Data Center/Server, auth flows, fountain codes, Android.
